item.title: France Records Hottest Day Ever as European Heat Wave Deepens
item.description: France recorded its hottest ever night and day as the heat wave searing western Europe deepened, disrupting schools, transport and tourist sites.

item.title: Africa’s Biggest Solar Mini-Grid Operator Sells Stake to Expand
item.description: WeLight, which operates more solar mini-grids in Africa than any other company, said the International Finance Corp. bought a stake in the business, backing its expansion into Nigeria and the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: Londoners Complain of ‘Unbearable Heat’ in City’s Modern Housing
item.description: In Britain’s capital city, an architectural trend that started about two decades ago is now being linked to unbearable levels of indoor heat.

item.title: El Niño Is Raising the Risk of Severe Haze in Southeast Asia
item.description: Widespread dryness and heat driven by El Niño will raise the risks of severe haze in Southeast Asia this year, according to a report by the Singapore Institute of International Affairs.

item.title: UK Climate Watchdog Urges Removal of Green Levies From Bills
item.description: The UK government must do more to lower electricity costs, including by removing green levies from energy bills, if it is to meet its net zero emissions target by mid-century, the country’s climate watchdog has said.

item.title: Exelon Urges US States to Ease Rules on Utility-Owned Generation
item.description: The head of one of the US’s largest utilities is pressing states across the country to allow it to own generation facilities as it seeks to capitalize on surging demand from data centers, arguing that such a move would help curb rising energy prices for consumers.

item.title: Trump Administration, Qatar Say EU Methane Rules Threaten Energy Security
item.description: The US, Qatar and other natural gas exporters are urging the European Union to ease some of its pending methane emissions rules, warning that the regulations could threaten the bloc’s energy security.

item.title: India Urges Crop Shift for Monsoon Planting as Rains Falter
item.description: India is urging farmers in rain-fed areas with limited irrigation to shift to less water-intensive crops such as pulses, oilseeds and millets as authorities prepare for a potentially weak monsoon that could weigh on agricultural output.

item.title: How Extreme Weather Impacts Consumer Prices Around the World
item.description: Economists are trying to trace the impact of global warming on inflation

item.title: Trump Seeks a US Nuclear Revival With $17.5 Billion in Loans
item.description: The Trump administration is trying to revive the moribund industry to build large nuclear power plants in the US by offering $17.5 billion in financing for utilities to order equipment for reactors designed by Westinghouse Electric Co.

item.title: Canada Backs $504 Million Loan for Indigenous Nuclear Stake
item.description: Canada will make a C$715 million ($504 million) loan guarantee to a group of seven First Nations so they can buy a stake in a large nuclear project east of Toronto, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Victory of Trump Ally Ends Colombia’s Crusade to Wean World Off Fossil Fuels
item.description: Outgoing President Gustavo Petro banned new drilling and urged the world to decarbonize. His successor, Abelardo de la Espriella, wants “fracking to the max.”

item.title: JPMorgan Says Extreme Heat Is Altering Energy Demand Patterns
item.description: As Europeans head into a period of dangerously high temperatures, a team of executives from JPMorgan Chase & Co. says that more frequent heat waves come with profound implications for energy demand.

item.title: CATL Chairman Zeng Sees EVs Becoming AI Token Factories
item.description: As electric vehicles become smarter and pack more powerful chips, they may handle work for artificial intelligence models in the future, according to Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Chairman Robin Zeng.

item.title: UN Unveils Proposal to Monitor AI’s Environmental Impact
item.description: United Nations Secretary General Antonio Guterres says it’s time to start measuring the impact that artificial intelligence is having on the climate and nature.

item.title: ‘Super’ El Niño Risks Rise as Pacific Ocean Heat Soars
item.description: Exceptionally warm sea surface temperatures in the Pacific Ocean are setting the stage for a potential “super” El Niño of record-breaking intensity.

item.title: Extreme Heat Shuts London Schools, Flash Floods Disrupt Trains
item.description: Extreme heat across the southern UK is shutting schools and disrupting travel, after overnight thunderstorms led to flash floods in parts of London.

item.title: Intensifying Heat Wave Threatens to Set Records Across Europe
item.description: A strengthening heat wave is threatening to break temperature records from France and the UK to Spain and Germany, posing risks to transport, power systems and public health.

item.title: Gas Flaring by Fossil Fuel Producers Rose in 2025, Says World Bank
item.description: More than 500 million tons of greenhouse gases a year are released, an amount that exceeds the UK’s annual emissions.

item.title: Mining Billionaire Calls on China to Push Green Ship Fuel Deal
item.description: China should be pushing to decarbonize global shipping fuel after plans to charge emissions fees stalled last year because of US opposition, according to Australian billionaire miner Andrew Forrest.

item.title: Australians Press UN Over Impact of Nation’s Fuel Exports
item.description: A group of Australian citizens has filed a complaint with a United Nations body arguing the climate change impacts of the nation’s fossil fuel exports are violating their human rights.

item.title: Los Angeles Warehouse Fire Smoke Chokes Southern California for Fifth Day
item.description: A fire at a cold storage warehouse near downtown Los Angeles continued pumping fumes across Southern California five days after it ignited, prompting emergency declarations as air quality reached unhealthy levels.

item.title: What Starmer’s Resignation Means for the UK’s Climate Goals
item.description: The government has faced growing pressure to loosen its commitment to cutting emissions.

item.title: Heat Wave Shows Why French Reactors Must Adapt to Climate Change
item.description: Global warming and extreme weather are forcing French nuclear reactors to adapt.

item.title: UK’s World-Leading Climate Goals Look Shakier in Post-Starmer Era
item.description: The British government faces significant pressure to make policy concessions on fossil fuels. It has stood firm, so far.

item.title: Twelve EU Nations Push to Boost Carbon Fund for Poorer States
item.description: A group of 12 European Union countries urged the bloc’s executive to bolster a fund financed through the carbon market which supports poorer member states in the transition away from fossil fuels.

item.title: Octopus Energy, CATL Announce Battery-Swapping Hubs for Trucks
item.description: UK energy supplier Octopus Energy Group Ltd. and Chinese battery manufacturer Contemporary Amperex Technology Co., or CATL, will build a European network of battery-swapping hubs for trucks, the two companies said on Monday.

item.title: What Climate Change Costs You at the Checkout
item.description: The higher temperatures climb, the more inflated household costs like groceries will become, economists predict.

item.title: Red Weather Warning Issued in UK for Extreme Heat and Humidity
item.description: Red weather warning issued for extreme heat and humidityBy Emily Beament and Rebecca Speare-Cole, Press Association

item.title: Climate Fund Guarantees Set to Woo $5 Billion for Mexico, Brazil
item.description: The Climate Investment Funds, a $14 billion pool of climate-focused capital, said it has approved funding for Brazil and Mexico that should help unlock billions of dollars in additional financing to speed up the decarbonization of their industrial sectors.
